title: ToolCapability
description: Structured description of a tool's behaviour. Replaces the v0 single-enum sideEffectLevel with the four attributes that actually matter for evaluation: reversibility, observability, blast radius, and sandbox availability.

reversibility.description: Can the side effect be undone? 'none' = no side effect; 'time-bounded-reversible' = reversible within some window (e.g. email drafts before send).

observability.description: Who sees that this tool ran? 'silent' = no trace; 'notified' = external parties are informed (e.g. emails sent).

scope.description: What surface is affected by this tool's side effects.

description.description: Freeform detail. 'Sends email to any address' vs 'Sends email only to verified tenant members' look alike by scope but differ by description.

fidelity.description: Quality of the sandbox. 'stub' = always-success mocks; 'recorded' = replays captured responses; 'real-isolated' = real tool against an isolated environment.

allowedInModes.description: Modes in which this tool may be invoked. Irreversible external tools typically forbid 'sandboxed-live' unless sandbox fidelity is 'real-isolated'.
